# Jordan Joe Cooper

> Engineering leader, builder and operator. Head of Engineering at Zumi. 10+ years building products, teams and technology organisations across fintech, payments and AI. Based in London, UK.

Jordan Joe Cooper is a Head of Engineering currently at Zumi, an early-stage AI startup for the hospitality industry. He joined as employee #1 in March 2024 and built the engineering organisation from scratch. Before that he spent four years at Moneyhub, progressing through Software Engineer, Tech Lead, Engineering Manager and Solutions Architect, delivering Open Banking APIs and payment products used by over 1.2 million consumers.

He writes about technology, engineering leadership and the nature of work. He has been referenced in Jon McNeill's Amazon bestseller "The Algorithm" (2025), created and hosted the "Diary of Humanity" podcast, and appeared on the DevLab Podcast.

## Pages

- [Home](https://jordanjoecooper.com/): Overview, recent writing, and current role
- [About](https://jordanjoecooper.com/about.html): Personal background, featured media, and hobby projects
- [Experience](https://jordanjoecooper.com/experience.html): Full work history and career timeline
- [Writing](https://jordanjoecooper.com/writing.html): All articles and essays

## Writing
<!-- LLMS-WRITING-START -->
- [Programming & Jevons Paradox](https://jordanjoecooper.com/posts/programming-jeavons-paradox.html): Why cheaper code may lead to more programmers, not fewer (2026-06-01)
- [Avoiding Persian Messenger Syndrome](https://jordanjoecooper.com/posts/avoiding-persian-messenger-syndrome.html): Many organisations have a news problem, good news is shared and bad news is buried, here's why and how to avoid it. (2026-03-04)
- [Work and artificial intelligence](https://jordanjoecooper.com/posts/work-and-artificial-intelligence.html): The modern printing press with speed boost. AI is changing what human work is and means. (2026-02-23)
- [Quotes / Aphorisms](https://jordanjoecooper.com/posts/aphorisms.html): Collection of quotes and aphorisms from books, conversations, and podcasts. Wisdom from Charlie Munger, Warren Buffettt, Marcus Aurelius, and others. (2025-08-14)
<!-- LLMS-WRITING-END -->

## Experience

- **Head of Engineering, Zumi** (Mar 2024–Present). Joined as employee #1. Built the engineering team and product from scratch. Multi-tenant SaaS platform on TypeScript, PostgreSQL, GCP, Terraform. Team of 8 engineers.
- **Solutions Architect / Engineering Manager / Tech Lead / Software Engineer, Moneyhub** (Feb 2020–Apr 2024). Promoted through four roles during rapid company growth. Led engineering on payments, mobile applications and Open Banking capabilities serving 1.2M+ consumers. Built one of the UK's first Open Banking payment widgets.
- **Software Developer, Nimble** (2018–2020). Built frontends, backend services, APIs and integrations for an e-learning platform.
- **Software Developer & Data Protection Officer, Kappu** (2017–2018). First engineering hire. Built the platform and established engineering processes.

## Skills & Expertise

Engineering Leadership · People Management · Hiring & Coaching · Organisation Design · Technical Strategy · Delivery Management · Distributed Systems · Multi-Tenant SaaS · Event-Driven Architecture · Open Banking · Payments · TypeScript · Node.js · PostgreSQL · GCP · Terraform · Serverless · Docker

## Education

BSc (Hons) Computing (2:1) · FdSc Computing — Canterbury Christ Church University (2014–2017)

## Featured

- Referenced in "The Algorithm" by Jon McNeill (Amazon bestseller, 2025), page 46, for the first product iteration built at Zumi
- Creator and host of "Diary of Humanity" podcast
- Guest on DevLab Podcast

## Hobby Projects

- [The Algorithm](https://github.com/jordanjoecooper/the-algorithm): AI skill implementing Jon McNeill's five-step framework (from Tesla and Lyft) for structured problem analysis
- [whenyoufree?](https://whenyoufree.app): Group scheduling — share one link, everyone taps the dates they're free, no accounts required
- [Don't Wait for Tomorrow](https://dontwaitfortomorrow.uk): Life expectancy calculator that visualises remaining time using mortality and time-use data by country
- [PlanahShop](https://www.etsy.com/shop/PlanahShop): Digital planners on Etsy
